patch.value:
# Generate completions, MCP tool registrations, and command docs from the contracts table

Make `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts` the single machine-consumed source of truth for every derived command surface: bash/zsh/fish completions, the MCP tool registration table, and the command reference docs. Today each of these is hand-maintained, which is why adding one command requires touching ~10 sites (commands/index.ts, register-mutation.ts, mcp/server.ts, cli-contracts names/actions/flag-contracts/schema-contracts/params, commands/contracts.ts, three completion scripts, docs, vitest.config.ts) and why contract drift keeps recurring (pm-zx13, pm-4os2, GH-449/pm-kjbh are all instances of the same class).

## Why now
Grammar principle 6 of this epic's charter says "help tiers, completions, MCP tools, and docs are all generated from the same contract table", but only the help tier has an implementing child (pm-e2bq). Doing this BEFORE the noun-verb consolidation lands means every consolidation child (pm-pfqi, pm-tqel, pm-tnud, …) updates one table instead of ten files, and the alias shim layer (pm-0z7n) gets its alias list from the same table.

## Scope
- Completions: generate bash/zsh/fish from the contract table at build time (checked-in generated output + a drift gate, matching the contracts:update pattern).
- MCP: derive tool registration (names, action mapping, input schemas) from PM_TOOL_ACTIONS + SCHEMA_CONTRACTS so a missing SCHEMA_CONTRACTS entry is a build-time error, not a `contracts --full` crash (PR#357 trap).
- Docs: generate the per-command reference skeleton (flags, defaults, aliases) into docs, keeping prose sections hand-written.
- Reduce the documented new-command wiring checklist to: implement handler + add one contracts entry + prose docs.

## Acceptance criteria
A drift gate fails CI when completions/MCP table/docs skeleton do not match the contract table; the new-command wiring checklist in docs shrinks to ≤3 manual sites; existing surfaces are byte-stable or intentionally improved (snapshot-reviewed).

## Out of scope
The help-tier renderer (pm-e2bq), the grammar/alias decisions themselves (ADR pm-pbyu), SDK promotion of contracts (covered by pm-usfg children).
Generate shell completions, MCP tool registrations, and command reference docs from the contracts table (single source of truth)

Implements grammar principle 6 of the pm-n7rr charter beyond the help tier (pm-e2bq): completions (bash/zsh/fish), the MCP tool table, and command reference doc skeletons become build-time generated from src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts with a CI drift gate, shrinking the ~10-site new-command wiring checklist to ≤3 sites and retiring the recurring contract-drift bug class (pm-zx13, pm-4os2, GH-449). Dedupe-checked 2026-07-06: only pm-e2bq (help tier) exists; no item covers completions/MCP/docs generation.

CI drift gate fails when completions/MCP table/docs skeleton diverge from the contract table; documented new-command wiring checklist shrinks to <=3 manual sites; existing surfaces byte-stable or intentionally improved via reviewed snapshots
